codeReview.summary: The commit adds substantial real integration coverage for non-TTY fallback, scope+target combinations, and no-language degradation, but it does not validate the interactive portions required by the feature contract for VAL-CROSS-004/005. Because those assertions are explicitly interactive, coverage remains incomplete.

issues.description: VAL-CROSS-004 requires an interactive cancel flow followed by a deterministic non-interactive rerun. The test explicitly substitutes this with a non-TTY guard failure (`runCliNonTTY(["install"])`) and never exercises interactive cancellation behavior, so the required flow is not actually validated.

VAL-CROSS-005 requires parity between interactive and non-interactive runs for the same intent. This section runs only non-interactive variants (`-y -n` vs `--all --dry-run`) and checks partial markers/line counts rather than equivalence against an interactive transcript, leaving the interactive parity assertion unproven.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill procedure still mandates manual interactive verification, but worker runs are non-interactive; guidance should explicitly define an accepted deterministic substitute for interactive-only assertions to avoid repeated VAL-CROSS-004/005 interpretation gaps.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/cli-refactor-worker/SKILL.md:27 requires manual interactive verification; mission AGENTS allows deterministic evidence alternative at /home/milton/.factory/missions/cce2edb7-2356-48d5-879a-6bf7342e4252/AGENTS.md:43; reviewed handoff only reports non-TTY command evidence at ...cross-flow-parity__eaca5989-16ea-4679-ab63-1712c63d50f8.json:46,51,56.

summary: Reviewed feature `rulesync-architecture-cross-flow-parity` using feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, and commit `e4e3098`. Result: FAIL due to blocking gaps in VAL-CROSS-004/005 evidence (interactive cancel/parity not actually exercised).
